example_usage:
Basic usage:

resource "cloudstack_vpc" "default" {
  name         = "test-vpc"
  cidr         = "10.0.0.0/16"
  vpc_offering = "Default VPC Offering"
  zone         = "zone-1"
}

argument_reference.name: display_text
argument_reference.description: The display text of the VPC.

argument_reference.name: cidr
argument_reference.description: The CIDR block for the VPC. Changing this forces a new resource to be created.

argument_reference.name: vpc_offering
argument_reference.description: The name or ID of the VPC offering to use for this VPC. Changing this forces a new resource to be created.

argument_reference.name: network_domain
argument_reference.description: The default DNS domain for networks created in this VPC. Changing this forces a new resource to be created.

argument_reference.name: project
argument_reference.description: The name or ID of the project to deploy this instance to. Changing this forces a new resource to be created.

argument_reference.name: zone
argument_reference.description: The name or ID of the zone where this disk volume will be available. Changing this forces a new resource to be created.

attributes_reference.name: display_text
attributes_reference.description: The display text of the VPC.

attributes_reference.name: source_nat_ip
attributes_reference.description: The source NAT IP assigned to the VPC.

import:
VPCs can be imported; use <VPC ID> as the import ID. For example:

terraform import cloudstack_vpc.default 84b23264-917a-4712-b8bf-cd7604db43b0When importing into a project you need to prefix the import ID with the project name:

terraform import cloudstack_vpc.default my-project/84b23264-917a-4712-b8bf-cd7604db43b0
